name: TPI COMPOSITES, INC
business: TPI Composites, Inc. is an independent manufacturer of composite wind blades for the wind energy market. It also provides field service inspection and repair services to OEM customers and wind farm owners. The company's manufacturing footprint is in the U.S., Mexico, and India.
sellsTo: original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in the wind energy market

exposures.sentence: We are an independent manufacturer of composite wind blades for the wind energy market with a manufacturing footprint currently in the U.S., Mexico, and India.

The global demand for clean energy continues to rise, driven by factors such as the growing need for data centers dedicated to artificial intelligence, semiconductor chip manufacturers, the adoption of electric vehicles, and the electrification of buildings.

catalysts.sentence: On August 11, 2025 (the Petition Date ), TPI Composites, Inc. and certain of its direct and indirect subsidiaries (such subsidiaries, together with the Company, collectively, the Company Parties or the Debtors ) each filed voluntary petitions for relief under chapter 11 of title 11 of the United States Bankruptcy Code
On August 19, 2025, the Company s common stock, par value $0.01 per share, was suspended from trading on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC ( Nasdaq ).

catalysts.event: Common stock began trading on OTC Pink
catalysts.sentence: The common stock began trading on the OTC Pink Market on August 19, 2025 under the symbol TPICQ.
On March 1, 2026, the Company received a letter from the lenders (the DIP Lenders ) under the DIP Credit Agreement regarding an Event of Default occurring under the DIP Credit Agreement

catalysts.event: DIP default waiver and maturity extension
catalysts.sentence: On March 16, 2026, the DIP Lenders agreed to waive the DIP Default, extend the maturity date under the DIP Credit Agreement and consent to the Vestas Sale Transactions, ECP Sale Transaction and GEV Transaction as set forth in more detail in the Oaktree Consent Term Sheet filed with the Bankruptcy Court.

catalysts.event: Agreement to sell India and Mexico facilities to Vestas
catalysts.sentence: On March 4, 2026, following a competitive marketing process to sell all or part of the Debtors asset pursuant to section 363 of the Bankruptcy Code, the Company and certain of its direct and indirect subsidiaries (collectively, the Sellers ), entered into various agreements with Vestas Wind Systems A/S and certain of its subsidiaries (collectively, Vestas ), pursuant to which the Company will sell and transfer its manufacturing business in Chennai, India for a purchase price of approximately $10.0 million, and its manufacturing business in Matamoros, Mexico for a purchase price of approximately $14.0 million (collectively, the Vestas Sale Transactions ).
if the Vestas Sale Transactions are not closed by June 30, 2026

catalysts.event: Agreement to sell U.S. and Mexico assets to ECP
catalysts.sentence: On March 6, 2026, the Company and certain of its direct and indirect subsidiaries (collectively, the ECP Seller Parties ) entered into a Stock and Asset Purchase Agreement (the ECP Purchase Agreement ) with ECP Blade Holdings LLC ( ECP Buyer ) ... in exchange for approximately $20.0 million in cash, subject to certain purchase price adjustments and the assumption of certain liabilities, each as set forth in the ECP Purchase Agreement (the ECP Sale Transaction ).
if the ECP Sale Transaction has not been consummated by June 30, 2026, subject to extension in certain circumstances.

catalysts.event: Agreement with GE Vernova as back-up bidder
catalysts.sentence: On March 16, 2026, the Company and certain of its direct and indirect subsidiaries (collectively, the Back-Up Bidder Seller Parties ) entered into a Term Sheet (the GE Vernova Term Sheet ) with GE Vernova International LLC ( GE Vernova ), setting forth the terms of definitive documentation ... in exchange for approximately $21.0 million in cash (the GE Vernova Transaction ).

catalysts.event: GE Vernova transaction back-up obligation deadline
catalysts.sentence: If the ECP Purchase Agreement is terminated and the GE Vernova Transaction is not consummated prior to August 31, 2026, GE Vernova is obligated to purchase the certain obligations of the DIP Lenders under the DIP Credit Agreement from the DIP Lenders.
our Newton, Iowa facility which restarted production in the second half of 2025
On September 10, 2025, the Company consummated the sale and transfer of 100% of its ownership interests of the Company s two Turkish subsidiaries
